subject: LU-14277 lod: statfs should not block a create

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

> what exact problem the patch is trying to solve?

I would guess this is trying to fix the same as https://review.whamcloud.com/40274 "LU-13073 osp: don't block waiting for new objects"?  If that is the case, it would be useful if it added the same test case from that patch.

Patch Set 1:

Andreas, osp_statfs() never blocks, thus statfs update in LOD shouldn't really block holding that semaphore

Patch Set 1:

Alex Zhuravlev, even if osp_statfs() is not blocked, down_write(&ltd->ltd_qos.lq_rw_sem) is a bottle neck for all creation threads above osfs_age. Looks like this semaphore doesn't protect anything and could be simplified.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Commit msg is outdated and has wrong LU number, ltd_foreach_tgt() should be protected with lod_getref(ltd).
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 3:

I would like to clear a bit this patch, I rewrite the original fix from Alexey Lyashkov, and I'm not putting +1 here. So Alexey review is valid.

Patch Set 3:

> I would like to clear a bit this patch, I rewrite the original fix
 > from Alexey Lyashkov, and I'm not putting +1 here. So Alexey review
 > is valid.

Sure, but the script that checks for patches ready to land is not smart enough to know this.  It does not count reviews from the patch owner to avoid accidental self-review.  So it is better for you to add a +1 on the patch, and preferably remove Alexey's +1 so it doesn't _look_ like there are two reviews when there is only one "valid" review that the script is checking.

commit.subject: LU-14277 lod: statfs should not block a create
commit.message:
LU-14277 lod: statfs should not block a create

lod_qos_statfs_update() need a guarantee that targets
isn't changed, so it doesn't need to take a QoS mutex.
